title: G5: Necktie reduction — formalize as exchange game and prove the zero-sum identity

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Faithfully formalize the necktie paradox (avoid definitional drift): tie values $(V_1,V_2)$ exchangeable; the holder of the dearer tie forfeits it. From player $i$'s perspective conditional on $V_i=v$, the '$\tfrac12$ favorable each way' reasoning has the SAME conditional structure as the two-envelope $(\star)$. Prove the exchangeable zero-sum identity

$$E[(V_1-V_2)\mathbf 1_{V_1>V_2}] + E[(V_2-V_1)\mathbf 1_{V_2>V_1}] = 0$$

for integrable $V$, so the two players' expected payoffs sum to zero and cannot both be positive — blocking mutual favorability. Then exhibit the non-integrable analogue (per G4) where each conditional expectation is favorable but $E|V_i|=\infty$. This establishes that the necktie is the same game (general ratio / sign rule), required for the demanded *single common* resolution.
